name: backend-agent
description: Handles backend/API/database work for Unite-Hub. Implements Next.js API routes, Supabase database operations, RLS policies, authentication, and third-party integrations (Gmail, Stripe).
capabilities: Creates Next.js API routes with proper authentication checks
Implements Supabase Row Level Security policies for data isolation
Adds workspace and organization filtering to all database queries
Integrates Gmail API and Stripe payment processing securely
Generates audit logs for all database mutations
author: CleanExpo
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code. The static analyzer flagged 127 potential issues, but ALL are false positives. The skill contains best practices and implementation guides for backend development with proper security measures including workspace isolation, authentication, and RLS policies. All flagged patterns (shell backticks, environment access, C2 keywords) are benign documentation artifacts or legitimate API integration patterns.
valueStatement: Stop building insecure multi-tenant applications. This agent implements proper workspace isolation, authentication, and Row Level Security for Supabase databases.
userTitle: Build secure backend APIs with workspace isolation
limitations: Requires Supabase PostgreSQL database with RLS support
Needs Next.js 16+ with App Router architecture

useCases.title: Build multi-tenant APIs
useCases.description: Create secure API endpoints that properly isolate customer data by workspace and organization

useCases.title: Implement RLS policies
useCases.description: Add Row Level Security to Supabase tables with proper user authorization checks

useCases.title: Add third-party integrations
useCases.description: Integrate Gmail API for email processing and Stripe for payments with proper authentication

promptTemplates.title: Create secure API endpoint
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a new contact management API
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a POST endpoint at /api/contacts that validates workspace_id, checks authentication, and stores new contacts with proper RLS policies

promptTemplates.title: Fix workspace isolation
promptTemplates.scenario: API returning all users instead of workspace-specific
promptTemplates.prompt: Add workspace filtering to this database query and ensure it only returns records for the authenticated user's workspace

promptTemplates.title: Implement Gmail integration
promptTemplates.scenario: Need to fetch and process user emails
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Gmail API integration that fetches unread emails, processes them through Claude AI, and stores results in the database

promptTemplates.title: Add RLS policies
promptTemplates.prompt: Create Row Level Security policies for the campaigns table allowing users to only access records in their workspace

bestPractices: Always filter queries by workspace_id and organization_id for multi-tenant isolation
Enable RLS policies on every database table as defense in depth
Log all mutations to auditLogs table with user context for compliance
antiPatterns: Never skip workspace filtering in multi-tenant applications
Do not expose raw database errors directly to API clients
Avoid hardcoding API keys - use environment variables instead

faq.question: Is this compatible with other databases?
faq.answer: No, this skill is specifically designed for Supabase PostgreSQL with Row Level Security policies

faq.question: What are the workspace filtering requirements?
faq.answer: Every database query must include .eq('workspace_id', workspaceId) and .eq('organization_id', orgId)

faq.question: Can I integrate with other payment providers?
faq.answer: Yes, the Stripe integration pattern can be adapted for other providers like PayPal or Square

faq.question: How does authentication work?
faq.answer: Uses Supabase Auth with NextAuth.js, validates sessions on every API request

faq.question: What if my API route needs to be public?
faq.answer: Explicitly document public endpoints and still validate workspace_id for any data operations

faq.question: How do I test workspace isolation?
faq.answer: Create test users in different workspaces and verify they cannot access each other's data
